state_variables.name: depositor
state_variables.semantic_gap: While Ethereum addresses are 20-byte cryptographic identifiers‚ Fabric identities are certificate-based X․509 DN strings․ These two identity models are architecturally incompatible

state_variables.name: beneficiary
state_variables.semantic_gap: Similar to the depositor‚ an Ethereum address cannot be directly mapped to Fabric X․509 identity

state_variables.name: arbiter
state_variables.semantic_gap: The Fabric X.509 identity cannot be simply translated to an Ethereum address

state_variables.name: balance
state_variables.semantic_gap: The implicit Ethereum storage slot must be replaced with explicit Fabric GetState and PutState calls with a specific key

state_variables.name: isApproved
state_variables.semantic_gap: Implicit Ethereum storage slots must be converted to explicit Fabric GetState and PutState calls with key

events.name: Deposited
events.semantic_gap: It is not possible to reproduce Ethereum permanent indexed event logs at the contract level in Fabric. Redesigning the application layer is necessary

portability_summary.note: The most comprehensive case study that addresses each of the five portability obstacles is escrow. At the contract level, PB-3 Ether transfer and PB-5 event queryability are not transferable
